title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: MHN(α\alpha, β\beta, γ\gamma) distribution support (0,∞)(0, \infty) density f(x∣α,β,γ)=2βα/2xα−1exp⁡(−βx2+γx)Ψ[α/2,γ/β],x>0,f(x \mid \alpha, \beta, \gamma) =   \frac{2 \beta^{\alpha/2} \, x^{\alpha-1} \, \exp(-\beta x^2 + \gamma x)}        {\Psi[\alpha/2,\, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]},   \qquad x > 0, α>0\alpha > 0, β>0\beta > 0, γ∈ℝ\gamma \\mathbb{R}, Ψ[,z]\Psi[, z] Fox–Wright 1Ψ1{}_1\Psi_1 function used normalising constant (Sun, Kong & Pal 2023). three parameters control polynomial factor xα−1x^{\alpha-1}, Gaussian tail exp⁡(−βx2)\exp(-\beta x^2), exponential tilt exp⁡(γx)\exp(\gamma x) respectively.

title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: dmhn() evaluates density (log-density) one points: vectorised x parameters, standard R recycling: MHN densities three parameter triples; steelblue curve sits alpha < 1, gamma >> 0 regime density combines boundary divergence x 0+ interior local maximum near x = 1.8 (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 3c). y-axis clipped 1; divergent left tails tomato steelblue curves continue upward beyond plot.
code: dmhn(c(0.5, 1, 2), alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) #> [1] 0.4702986 0.7325378 0.1982764 dmhn(1, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, log = TRUE) #> [1] -0.3112403 dmhn(c(0.5, 1, 1.5), alpha = c(1, 2), beta = 1, gamma = c(0, 1, -1)) #> [1] 0.87878258 0.73253783 0.04310111 x <- c(seq(0.001, 0.3, length.out = 60), seq(0.3, 5, length.out = 140)) plot(x, dmhn(x, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1),      type = "l", lwd = 2, ylab = "density", ylim = c(0, 1),      main = expression("MHN(" * alpha * ", " * beta * ", " * gamma * ")")) lines(x, dmhn(x, alpha = 0.5, beta = 1, gamma = 1), lwd = 2, col = "tomato") lines(x, dmhn(x, alpha = 0.3, beta = 1, gamma = 4), lwd = 2, col = "steelblue") legend("topright", bty = "n",        legend = c("(2, 1, 1)    log-concave, mode near 1",                   "(0.5, 1, 1)  monotone decreasing",                   "(0.3, 1, 4)  boundary spike + interior bump"),        col = c("black", "tomato", "steelblue"), lwd = 2)

title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: pmhn() returns P(X≤q)P(X \le q) (complement / log-probability via lower.tail / log.p). general case evaluates Sun et al. (2023) Lemma 1b series log space, truncated constructive bound K=max⁡{K1,K2}K = \max\{K_1, K_2\} Sun et al. (2023, Supplementary Lemma 10(d)). underlying double-precision cancellation alternating-sign accumulator γ<0\gamma < 0 exceed user’s tolerance, routine falls back peak-normalised Boost.Math quadrature (Gauss–Kronrod α≥1\alpha \ge 1, tanh–sinh α<1\alpha < 1) unnormalised density. direct cross-check integrate(dmhn, 0, q) confirms accuracy: Density matching CDF MHN(2, 1, 1).
code: pmhn(c(0.5, 1, 1.5, 2), alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) #> [1] 0.1129101 0.4338796 0.7614259 0.9363038 pmhn(2, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.06369619 pmhn(2, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, log.p = TRUE) #> [1] -0.06581527 q <- 1.5 ref <- integrate(function(x) dmhn(x, 2, 1, 1), 0, q,                  rel.tol = 1e-10)$value all.equal(pmhn(q, 2, 1, 1), ref) #> [1] TRUE plot(x, dmhn(x, 2, 1, 1), type = "l", lwd = 2, ylab = "f(x)", main = "Density") plot(x, pmhn(x, 2, 1, 1), type = "l", lwd = 2, ylab = "F(x)", main = "CDF",      ylim = c(0, 1))

title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: qmhn() inverts CDF using TOMS 748 root-finder bracketed [ϵ,E(X)+8Var(X)][\sqrt{\epsilon}, E(X) + 8 \sqrt{\mathrm{Var}(X)}], expanded required. round-trip identity holds within inverter’s tolerance: lower.tail log.p follow conventions qnorm/qgamma:

title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: rmhn(n, alpha, beta, gamma) draws n variates. default method = "auto" chooses special-case shortcuts (sqrt-Gamma γ=0\gamma = 0; truncated normal α=1\alpha = 1), Sun et al. (2023) Algorithms 1 / 3, Gao & Wang (2025) Relaxed Transformed Density Rejection (RTDR) sampler. user can force single sampler via method = "rtdr" method = "sun". 10,000 draws MHN(2, 1, 1) true density overlaid. Switching method useful benchmarking; seed forced paths produce statistically equivalent samples:
code: rmhn(5, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) #> [1] 0.3705190 1.1166586 0.9019525 1.3946419 1.1552967  # Vector parameters are recycled to length n. rmhn(5, alpha = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)) #> [1] 0.2046802 0.5574992 0.9775332 0.7787843 1.8248242 set.seed(42) draws <- rmhn(10000, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) hist(draws, breaks = 60, probability = TRUE, col = "grey90", border = "white",      xlab = "x", main = "rmhn(10000, 2, 1, 1)") lines(x, dmhn(x, 2, 1, 1), lwd = 2, col = "tomato") set.seed(1); s_rtdr <- rmhn(5000, 2, 1, 1, method = "rtdr") set.seed(1); s_sun  <- rmhn(5000, 2, 1, 1, method = "sun") ks.test(s_rtdr, s_sun)$p.value #> [1] 0.3274975

title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: package provides closed-form / recurrence-based helpers common summary statistics (Sun et al. 2023, Lemmas 2 3): interior mode exists (e.g. α<1\alpha < 1 γ≤0\gamma \le 0), mhn_mode() returns NA:
code: data.frame(   Quantity = c("mean", "variance", "skewness", "excess kurtosis", "mode"),   Function = c("mhn_mean", "mhn_var", "mhn_skewness",                "mhn_kurtosis", "mhn_mode"),   Value = c(     mhn_mean(2, 1, 1),     mhn_var(2, 1, 1),     mhn_skewness(2, 1, 1),     mhn_kurtosis(2, 1, 1),     mhn_mode(2, 1, 1)   ) ) #>          Quantity     Function       Value #> 1            mean     mhn_mean 1.133731086 #> 2        variance      mhn_var 0.281519367 #> 3        skewness mhn_skewness 0.463887428 #> 4 excess kurtosis mhn_kurtosis 0.006868473 #> 5            mode     mhn_mode 1.000000000 mhn_mode(0.5, 1, -1) #> [1] NA

title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: MHN family contains several familiar distributions: package detects case (within sqrt(.Machine$double.eps)) dispatches corresponding closed-form R primitive, dmhn / pmhn / qmhn / rmhn exact regimes: MHN(1, 1, 1) equivalent truncated normal density.
code: # gamma = 0: dmhn matches the change-of-variable sqrt-Gamma density. xx <- c(0.5, 1, 1.5, 2) mhn_d  <- dmhn(xx, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 0) ref_d  <- dgamma(xx^2, shape = 1, rate = 1) * 2 * xx all.equal(mhn_d, ref_d) #> [1] TRUE mu_tn    <- 1 / (2 * 1) sigma_tn <- 1 / sqrt(2 * 1) tn_dens  <- function(x) {   dnorm(x, mu_tn, sigma_tn) / pnorm(0, mu_tn, sigma_tn, lower.tail = FALSE) } plot(x, dmhn(x, 1, 1, 1), type = "l", lwd = 2, ylab = "density",      main = "alpha = 1: MHN reduces to truncated normal") points(x, tn_dens(x), pch = ".", col = "tomato", cex = 2) legend("topright", bty = "n",        legend = c("dmhn(x, 1, 1, 1)", "TN reference"),        col = c("black", "tomato"), lwd = c(2, NA), pch = c(NA, 19))

title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: See ?dmhn, ?pmhn, ?qmhn, ?rmhn full argument documentation, including recycling rules method argument rmhn. citation("mhn") lists package two underlying papers. Sun, Kong & Pal (2023) develop parametric family Algorithms 1 3 used . Gao & Wang (2025) introduce RTDR sampler uniform 1/e1/e acceptance.

title: Introduction to the mhn Package
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics – Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536. Gao, F. & Wang, H.-B. (2025). Generating modified-half-normal random variates relaxed transformed density rejection method. Communications Statistics – Simulation Computation. Robert, C. P. (1995). Simulation truncated normal variables. Statistics Computing, 5(2), 121–125.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: vignette theoretical companion Introduction mhn Package. Introduction shows four exported functions (dmhn / pmhn / qmhn / rmhn) moment helpers ; note develops mathematical algorithmic theory package implements. summarise Modified Half-Normal (MHN) family, Fox–Wright Ψ\Psi function appears normalising constant, two sampling schemes package draws — Sun, Kong & Pal (2023) Gao & Wang (2025). deliberately keep treatment compact. Full proofs finer numerical considerations live original papers (see References). Two implementation-relevant points — typesetting errata Sun et al. (2023) package silently corrects, benchmark-driven rationale rmhn(method = "auto") crossover 25 — inserted §4 §7 relevant.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: MHN distribution support (0,∞)(0, \infty) density f(x∣α,β,γ)=2βα/2xα−1exp⁡(−βx2+γx)Ψ[α/2,γ/β],x>0,f(x \mid \alpha, \beta, \gamma) =   \frac{2 \beta^{\alpha/2} \, x^{\alpha-1} \, \exp(-\beta x^2 + \gamma x)}        {\Psi[\alpha/2,\, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]},   \qquad x > 0, α>0\alpha > 0, β>0\beta > 0, γ∈ℝ\gamma \\mathbb{R}. three parameters control three independent factors density kernel: Three familiar distributions sit inside family exact special cases (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 6): also degenerate boundary limit, listed table: β→0+\beta \0^+ γ<0\gamma < 0, MHN density converges Gamma(α,−γ)\mathrm{Gamma}(\alpha,\, -\gamma). package handle limit specially β>0\beta > 0 required MHN parameter space; Gamma draw β=0\beta = 0, use stats::rgamma directly. three finite special cases table — γ=0\gamma = 0, α=1\alpha = 1, together — package detects constraint within sqrt(.Machine$double.eps) tolerance dispatches corresponding closed-form primitive stats, four exported functions (dmhn / pmhn / qmhn / rmhn) exact three regimes, bypassing general series--rejection machinery. MHN density alpha crosses log-concavity threshold; beta = 1, gamma = 1 fixed. figure illustrates qualitative claims developed next section. density log-concave whenever α≥1\alpha \geq 1 (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 3a). α>1\alpha > 1 finite interior mode closed form (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 3b); borderline case α=1\alpha = 1 reduces truncated normal, whose mode max⁡(0,γ/(2β))\max(0,\, \gamma/(2\beta)) — interior γ>0\gamma > 0, boundary x=0x = 0 otherwise. α<1\alpha < 1 density typically monotone decreasing boundary singularity x→0+x \0^+. one exception γ>0\gamma > 0 together α≥1−γ2/(8β)\alpha \geq 1 - \gamma^2/(8\beta), density unimodal: diverges x→0+x \0^+, dips local minimum, rises single interior local maximum, decays (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 3c). (β,γ)=(1,1)(\beta, \gamma) = (1, 1) threshold 1−1/8=0.8751 - 1/8 = 0.875, orange (α=0.5\alpha = 0.5) curve figure sits strict monotone-decreasing regime, interior local maximum.
code: x  <- seq(0.001, 4, length.out = 400) plot(x, dmhn(x, alpha = 2,   beta = 1, gamma = 1), type = "l", lwd = 2,      ylim = c(0, 1.4), ylab = "f(x)",      main = "MHN density at beta = 1, gamma = 1") lines(x, dmhn(x, alpha = 5,   beta = 1, gamma = 1), lwd = 2, col = "steelblue") lines(x, dmhn(x, alpha = 1,   beta = 1, gamma = 1), lwd = 2, col = "tomato") lines(x, dmhn(x, alpha = 0.5, beta = 1, gamma = 1), lwd = 2, col = "darkorange") legend("topright", bty = "n", lwd = 2,        col = c("darkorange", "tomato", "black", "steelblue"),        legend = c("alpha = 0.5  (no interior mode)",                   "alpha = 1    (truncated normal)",                   "alpha = 2    (log-concave)",                   "alpha = 5    (log-concave, shifted right)"))

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: Three facts Sun et al. (2023) drive everything package shape, moments, mode. Log-concavity function α\alpha alone (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 3a). α≥1\alpha \geq 1 density log-concave irrespective β\beta γ\gamma; α<1\alpha < 1 . single threshold splits Gao & Wang (2025) RTDR sampler log-axis original-axis branches §6 . mode closed form α>1\alpha > 1 (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 3b): Xmode=γ+γ2+8β(α−1)4β.X_{\mathrm{mode}}  = \frac{\gamma + \sqrt{\gamma^2 + 8\beta(\alpha - 1)}}{4\beta}. α=1\alpha = 1 mode max⁡(0,γ/(2β))\max(0,\, \gamma/(2\beta)) (truncated-normal mode, Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 6b). α<1\alpha < 1 density either monotone decreasing develops non-trivial local maximum local minimum, depending sign 1−γ2/(8β)−α1 - \gamma^2/(8\beta) - \alpha (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 3c–d). helper mhn_mode() implements case split returns NA interior mode exists. Moments satisfy two-term recurrence (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 2b): E(Xk+2)=α+k2βE(Xk)+γ2βE(Xk+1).E(X^{k+2})  = \frac{\alpha + k}{2 \beta}\, E(X^{k})  + \frac{\gamma}{2 \beta}\, E(X^{k+1}). Together closed-form ratio E(X)E(X) terms Ψ\Psi (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 2a), gives every higher moment. helpers mhn_mean(), mhn_var(), mhn_skewness() mhn_kurtosis() apply recurrence C++ via Rcpp; Introduction vignette shows action. useful sanity bound (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 4c) Var(X)≤1/(2β)\mathrm{Var}(X) \leq 1/(2\beta) every α≥1\alpha \geq 1, γ∈ℝ\gamma \\mathbb{R}.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: normalising constant MHN density Ψ[α2,z]=∑n=0∞Γ(α+n2)n!zn,z=γβ.\Psi\!\left[\frac{\alpha}{2},\, z\right]  \;=\; \sum_{n=0}^{\infty}         \frac{\Gamma\!\big(\tfrac{\alpha + n}{2}\big)}{n!} \, z^{n},  \qquad z = \frac{\gamma}{\sqrt{\beta}}. series absolutely convergent every α>0\alpha > 0 every z∈ℝz \\mathbb{R}, strictly positive. depends (α,β,γ)(\alpha, \beta, \gamma) two-dimensional combination (α,z)(\alpha, z). useful organisational point: Ψ\Psi matters package’s exported functions. practical consequence numerical issues evaluating Ψ\Psi — accumulated rounding lgamma, truncation error series — can affect d/p/q moment helpers; leak sampler. evaluation, package follows case split Sun et al. (2023, Lemma 9–11): γ=0\gamma = 0 gives Ψ[α/2,0]=Γ(α/2)\Psi[\alpha/2, 0] = \Gamma(\alpha/2). α=1\alpha = 1, α=2,γ≥0\alpha = 2,\, \gamma \geq 0 corner, reduce closed forms involving Φ\Phi (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 9c). γ>0\gamma > 0 uses alternating-free series Sun et al. (2023, Lemma 10) pre-computed truncation point. terms positive, catastrophic cancellation. γ<0\gamma < 0 switches integral representation (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 11) uses Boost’s gauss_kronrod (α≥1\alpha \geq 1) tanh_sinh (α<1\alpha < 1, boundary singularity uα−1u^{\alpha-1} needs double-exponential rule).

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: published paper two typesetting errors touch implementation. corrected supplementary derivation, package follows corrected form. Mixture weights pip_{}Lemma 7. main-text statement Lemma 7 gives weights Gamma\sqrt{\mathrm{Gamma}} mixture representation pi=12βα/2⋅Γ((α+1+)/2)(γ/β)ii!Ψ[α/2,γ/β]p_{} = \tfrac{1}{2\beta^{\alpha/2}} \cdot  \tfrac{\Gamma((\alpha + 1 + )/2)\, (\gamma/\sqrt{\beta})^{}}       {!\, \Psi[\alpha/2,\, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]}. sum one, contradicting definition Ψ\Psi Poisson–Gamma hierarchical representation Sun et al. (2023, Lemma 5a). correct form, reproduced supplementary proof Lemma 7 (Supplementary §2.13), pi=Γ((α+)/2)(γ/β)ii!Ψ[α/2,γ/β]p_{} \;=\;  \frac{\Gamma((\alpha + )/2)\, (\gamma/\sqrt{\beta})^{}}       {!\, \Psi[\alpha/2,\, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]} .e. drop leading 1/(2βα/2)1/(2\beta^{\alpha/2}) use α+\alpha + Γ\Gamma argument instead α+1+\alpha + 1 + . affects Algorithm 2 , package implement; correction recorded completeness. Series truncation discriminants C1C_{1}, C2C_{2}Lemma 10. truncation point Ψ\Psi series smallest kk (k+1)/(k)≤qA(k+1)/(k) \leq q (analogous bound BB). Reducing quadratic kk gives discriminant whose last term αx2\alpha x^{2} ((α+1)x2(\alpha+1) x^{2} C2C_{2}). main-text statement Lemma 10 prints αx\alpha x (α+1)x(\alpha+1) x — single missing power xx. supplementary derivation (Supplementary §2.2.1, §2.2.2) carries z2z^{2} throughout, agreement rederivation. corrected x2x^{2} form package uses src/mhn_psi_series.cpp (marked // ERRATA comment); evaluating Ψ\Psi published xx form give -small truncation point silently lose precision.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: Sun et al. (2023) propose three Accept–Reject schemes, indexed sign γ\gamma coarse split α\alpha. Two — Algorithm 1 Algorithm 3 — implemented package reachable via rmhn(method = "sun"). Algorithm 2 , section explains reason.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: parameter triple, Algorithm 1 constructs two proposal distributions — Normal mean μopt\mu_{\mathrm{opt}} variance 1/(2β)1/(2\beta), Gamma(α/2,δopt)\sqrt{\mathrm{Gamma}(\alpha/2,\, \delta_{\mathrm{opt}})} — multiplicative envelope constant K1K_{1} K2K_{2}. Setup chooses whichever envelope tighter (K1≤K2K_{1} \leq K_{2} reverse). two optima μopt\mu_{\mathrm{opt}} δopt\delta_{\mathrm{opt}} closed forms (Sun et al. 2023, Theorem 1b), setup cheap. acceptance probability bounded 0.8 α≥4\alpha \geq 4 (Sun et al. 2023, Theorem 2e). 1≤α<41 \leq \alpha < 4 uniform lower bound proved, original Figure 2 shows empirically high acceptance throughout range.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: tilt non-positive, Theorem 3 Sun et al. (2023) supplies proposal kernel based –GM-type inequality. tuning point m>0m > 0 exponent r=(βm+|γ|)/(2βm+|γ|)r = (\beta m + |\gamma|)/(2\beta m + |\gamma|), candidate T∼Gamma(α⋅r,m(βm+|γ|)),X=m⋅Tr.T \sim \mathrm{Gamma}\!\big(\alpha \cdot r,\, m(\beta m + |\gamma|)\big), \qquad X = m \cdot T^{r}. construction works every α>0\alpha > 0; uniform acceptance bound ≥1/2≈0.707\geq 1/\sqrt{2} \approx 0.707 (Sun et al. 2023, Theorem 4c) proved α>1\alpha > 1, procedure also samples correctly α≤1\alpha \leq 1. matching point initialised closed-form heuristic Sun et al. (2023, §4.2): minit=α2/(1+α)m_{\mathrm{init}} = \alpha^{2}/(1 + \alpha) α≤1.1\alpha \leq 1.1 elaborate expression based mode rightmost inflection point α>1.1\alpha > 1.1. package applies single Newton-Raphson refinement step regimes, producing mrecommendm_{\mathrm{recommend}}, whose acceptance probability within 0.2%0.2\% optimum across parameter range tabulated Sun et al. (2023, Table 1).

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: Algorithm 2 targets γ>0\gamma > 0 α<1\alpha < 1 via mixture representation fMHN(x∣α,β,γ)=∑=0∞pifi(x∣α,β),fi∼Gamma((α+)/2,β).f_{\mathrm{MHN}}(x \mid \alpha, \beta, \gamma)  \;=\; \sum_{=0}^{\infty} p_{}\, f_{}(x \mid \alpha, \beta), \qquad f_{} \;\sim\; \sqrt{\mathrm{Gamma}\!\big((\alpha + )/2,\, \beta\big)}. mixture-component sampler needs truncation point M†M^{\dagger} chosen tail mass beyond M†M^{\dagger} target tolerance (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 8). large γ2/β\gamma^{2}/\beta truncation grows like γ2/(ε2β)\gamma^{2}/(\varepsilon^{2} \beta), whole scheme degrades catastrophically — Gao & Wang (2025, Table 1) report slowdown ∼3×104×\sim 3 \times 10^{4}\times RTDR extreme case (α,γ)=(0.01,10000)(\alpha, \gamma) = (0.01, 10000). package therefore declines implement Algorithm 2 routes (α<1,γ>0)(\alpha < 1, \gamma > 0) RTDR, acceptance bound ≥1/e\geq 1/e (next section) holds uniformly.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: Gao & Wang (2025) introduce Relaxed Transformed Density Rejection (RTDR) sampler comes uniform lower bound acceptance probability across entire parameter space. Three ideas drive construction.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: Standard TDR builds piecewise-linear envelopes top log-transformed density. RTDR generalises TcT_{c} family Tc[x]=sign(c)⋅xc(c>−1,c≠0),T0[x]=log⁡x.T_{c}[x] = \mathrm{sign}(c) \cdot x^{c} \quad (c > -1,\, c \neq 0), \qquad T_{0}[x] = \log x. density ff TcT_{c}-concave Tc[f]T_{c}[f] concave. Logarithmic concavity (usual TDR setting) c=0c = 0 special case. package’s RTDR implementation uses c=−1/2c = -1/2 log-axis density α<1\alpha < 1 branches T−1/2[x]=−x−1/2T_{-1/2}[x] = -x^{-1/2} accommodates slower decay g(y)g(y) original ff boundary singularity x=0x = 0.

what: Working on the log axis when α<1\alpha < 1
title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: α<1\alpha < 1 density f(x∣α,γ)f(x \mid \alpha, \gamma) (0,∞)(0, \infty) blows left endpoint amenable direct envelope construction. change variable y=log⁡xy = \log x yields g(y∣α,γ)=exp⁡(αy−e2y+γey),y∈ℝ,g(y \mid \alpha, \gamma)  \;=\; \exp\!\big( \alpha y - e^{2y} + \gamma e^{y} \big), \qquad y \\mathbb{R}, bounded unimodal (Gao & Wang 2025, Lemma 3.3). RTDR operates gg α<1\alpha < 1 ff directly α≥1\alpha \geq 1.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: scale normalisation βX→X\sqrt{\beta} X \X (may assume β=1\beta = 1), RTDR envelope construction splits four regions based (α,γ)(\alpha, \gamma). γ\gamma table normalised tilt γnorm=γ/β\gamma_{\mathrm{norm}} = \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}; general β\beta package’s rmhn(method = "rtdr") rescales internally compares γnorm\gamma_{\mathrm{norm}} boundary 2(1−1−2α)2(1 - \sqrt{1 - 2\alpha}). Region () easiest: ff log-concave, two tangent lines constant segment suffice. Regions (b) (c) reuse T−1/2T_{-1/2} envelope gg, validity argument differing. Region (d) hardest — gg inflection point envelope needs KK secant segments log-convex side, KK growing logarithmically γ\gamma.

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: classical TDR construction asks optimal tangent contact points, generally closed form must found Newton iteration. Gao & Wang (2025, Theorem 2.1) show acceptance bound ≤e≈2.719\leq e \approx 2.719 (equivalently, acceptance probability ≥1/e≈0.368\geq 1/e \approx 0.368) holds long contact points lie anywhere inside fixed interval — namely log⁡f(m)f(t)∈{[0.46,2.49]f log-concave (region ()),[0.93,1.99]gT−1/2-concave (regions (b), (c)).\log\!\frac{f(m)}{f(t)} \ \begin{cases}    [0.46,\, 2.49] & f \text{ log-concave (region ())}, \\    [0.93,\, 1.99] & g \text{ $T_{-1/2}$-concave (regions (b), (c))}.  \end{cases} Setup therefore handful cheap iterations terminate soon bracket hit, need precise Newton solution. main practical edge RTDR Algorithms 1 / 3 Sun et al. (2023) Gibbs-style workloads (α,β,γ)(\alpha, \beta, \gamma) changes every iteration: setup cost amortised single draw instead many, cheap setup wins. Composing bounds Gao & Wang (2025, Theorems 3.1, 3.2, 4.4) yields headline guarantee RTDR: every (α,β,γ)(\alpha, \beta, \gamma) α>0\alpha > 0, β>0\beta > 0, γ∈ℝ\gamma \\mathbb{R}, acceptance probability least 1/e≈0.3681/e \approx 0.368. three algorithms Sun et al. (2023) parameter-dependent guarantees (parts parameter space — see §5). quick sanity check RTDR sampler converges right distribution four regions — empirical means RTDR vs theoretical means across regions (), (b), (c), (d), based 5000 draws: four rows hit right means two--three decimal places — sampling tolerance n=5000n = 5000.
code: cases <- data.frame(   region = c("(a)", "(b)", "(c)", "(d)"),   alpha  = c(2.0,    0.7,    0.3,    0.3),   gamma  = c(0.5,    1.0,    0.5,    5.0) ) set.seed(42) empirical <- mapply(   function(a, g) mean(rmhn(5000, alpha = a, beta = 1, gamma = g, method = "rtdr")),   cases$alpha, cases$gamma ) theoretical <- mapply(mhn_mean, cases$alpha, beta = 1, cases$gamma) data.frame(cases,            empirical_mean   = round(empirical,   4),            theoretical_mean = round(theoretical, 4)) #>   region alpha gamma empirical_mean theoretical_mean #> 1    (a)   2.0   0.5         1.0126           1.0016 #> 2    (b)   0.7   1.0         0.6442           0.6424 #> 3    (c)   0.3   0.5         0.2972           0.2823 #> 4    (d)   0.3   5.0         2.3321           2.3221

title: Theoretical background of the mhn package
text: rmhn exposes three values method: "sun" (force Sun Algorithm 1 / 3 path), "rtdr" (force Gao & Wang RTDR path), "auto" (default). "auto" route package’s recommendation; composes four shortcuts one decision tree (Step 3.7.3 final form, confirmed benchmarks inst/benchmarks/): Write SS samples_per_setup quantity last branch, S=max⁡(1,nmax⁡(Lα,Lβ,Lγ)),S \;=\; \max\!\Big(1,\; \frac{n}{\max(L_{\alpha},\, L_{\beta},\, L_{\gamma})}\Big), LαL_{\alpha}, LβL_{\beta}, LγL_{\gamma} recycled lengths three parameter vectors. intuition follows decomposing expected time per draw E[time/draw]=Tsetupn+Creject×Tper-proposal,E[\text{time}/\text{draw}]  \;=\; \frac{T_{\text{setup}}}{n}        \;+\; C_{\text{reject}} \times T_{\text{per-proposal}}, TsetupT_{\text{setup}} one-cost preparing proposal (single minitm_{\text{init}} formula plus one Newton step Sun Algorithm 3; iterative contact-point search RTDR), Creject=1/acceptanceC_{\text{reject}} = 1/\text{acceptance} expected number proposals per accepted draw, Tper-proposalT_{\text{per-proposal}} cost one proposal cycle (sample plus acceptance log-ratio evaluation). γ<0\gamma < 0 two samplers sit opposite ends trade-: Sun Algorithm 3 cheap setup — minitm_{\text{init}} closed form, optional Newton refinement uses one boost::math::digamma evaluation — expensive per-proposal: every proposal calls rgamma, evaluates fractional power X=m⋅TrX = m \cdot T^{r}, acceptance log-ratio carries (X/m)1/r(X/m)^{1/r} term. RTDR (region ()) expensive setup: contact point tlt_{l}, trt_{r} found iterative search terminates soon log⁡f(m)/f(t)\log f(m)/f(t) lands bracket [0.46,2.49][0.46,\, 2.49]. per-proposal light: sampling piecewise envelope straight arithmetic plus single log, acceptance ratio adds two logs. SS large per-proposal term dominates RTDR wins; small — Gibbs case iteration redraws (α,β,γ)(\alpha, \beta, \gamma) — setup term dominates Sun Algorithm 3 wins. crossover 25 round value chosen inside empirical break-even window n∈[10,25]n \[10,\, 25] observed every one 20 surveyed (α,γ)(\alpha, \gamma) cells inst/benchmarks/auto_dispatch.R. small concrete demonstration: set.seed aligned, method = "auto" expected forced method produce bit-identical draws branch. three TRUE outputs invariant tests/testthat/test-rmhn.R enforces regression test.
code: rmhn(n, alpha, beta, gamma, method = "auto")                                     │                                     ▼        ┌─ alpha = 1, gamma = 0  →  half-normal       (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 6c)        │        ├─ gamma = 0             →  sqrt-Gamma        (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 6a)        │        ├─ alpha = 1             →  truncated normal  (Sun et al. 2023, Lemma 6b)        │        ├─ alpha < 1 and gamma > 0        │      →  RTDR  (regions (b) / (c) / (d), Gao & Wang 2025, Theorems 3.2, 4.4)        │        ├─ alpha > 1 and gamma > 0        │      →  Sun Algorithm 1                    (Sun et al. 2023, Theorems 1, 2)        │        └─ gamma < 0               ├─ samples_per_setup ≥ 25  →  RTDR    (region (a))               └─ samples_per_setup < 25  →  Sun Algorithm 3 # Branch: alpha < 1 and gamma > 0  -->  expects RTDR set.seed(7); auto1 <- rmhn(20, alpha = 0.7, beta = 1, gamma = 2) set.seed(7); rtdr1 <- rmhn(20, alpha = 0.7, beta = 1, gamma = 2, method = "rtdr") identical(auto1, rtdr1) #> [1] TRUE  # Branch: alpha > 1 and gamma > 0  -->  expects Sun Algorithm 1 set.seed(7); auto2 <- rmhn(20, alpha = 3,   beta = 1, gamma = 2) set.seed(7); sun2  <- rmhn(20, alpha = 3,   beta = 1, gamma = 2, method = "sun") identical(auto2, sun2) #> [1] TRUE  # Branch: gamma < 0 with samples_per_setup < 25  -->  expects Sun Algorithm 3 set.seed(7); auto3 <- rmhn(5,  alpha = 2,   beta = 1, gamma = -1) set.seed(7); sun3  <- rmhn(5,  alpha = 2,   beta = 1, gamma = -1, method = "sun") identical(auto3, sun3) #> [1] TRUE

title: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution
text: R package providing density, distribution, quantile, random generation functions Modified Half-Normal (MHN) distribution, together closed-form / recurrence-based helpers moments mode.

title: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution
text: MHN(α\alpha, β\beta, γ\gamma) distribution support (0,∞)(0, \infty) density f(x∣α,β,γ)∝xα−1exp⁡(−βx2+γx),x>0,f(x \mid \alpha, \beta, \gamma) \;\propto\;   x^{\alpha - 1} \exp(-\beta x^2 + \gamma x), \qquad x > 0, α,β>0\alpha, \beta > 0 γ∈ℝ\gamma \\mathbb{R}. arises conditional posterior Bayesian MCMC several common models (skew-elliptical regression, tt-shrinkage priors, log-concave likelihoods quadratic Bayesian penalty) generalises number familiar one-sided distributions: package implements efficient samplers Sun, Kong & Pal (2023) (Algorithms 1 / 3) Gao & Wang (2025) Relaxed Transformed Density Rejection (RTDR) method uniform 1/e acceptance bound, dispatches automatically.

title: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution
text: package CRAN also installable usual
code: # Development version from GitHub: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("t-momozaki/mhn") install.packages("mhn")

title: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution
code: library(mhn)  # Density, CDF, quantile, random generation dmhn(c(0.5, 1, 2), alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) pmhn(1.5,           alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) qmhn(0.95,          alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) rmhn(10,            alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1)  # Summary statistics mhn_mean(2, 1, 1); mhn_var(2, 1, 1); mhn_mode(2, 1, 1)

title: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution
code: dmhn(1.5, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, log = TRUE) pmhn(1.5, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, lower.tail = FALSE) qmhn(log(0.05), alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, log.p = TRUE) rmhn(5, alpha = c(1, 2, 3), beta = 1, gamma = c(0, 1, -1))  # recycled

title: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution
code: mhn_mean(2, 1, 1)       # 1.16... mhn_skewness(2, 1, 1)   # positive (density right-skewed) mhn_mode(0.5, 1, -1)    # NA: monotone-decreasing density

title: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution
text: default method routes parameter triple cheapest sampler provably correct region. decision rules benchmarked inst/benchmarks/auto_dispatch.R: Forcing specific sampler:
code: Closed-form shortcuts:   α = 1, γ = 0    -> half-normal via |rnorm|   γ = 0           -> sqrt(rgamma(...))   α = 1           -> truncated normal  Region α < 1, γ > 0:   Gao & Wang (2025) RTDR  (Sun et al. (2023) Algorithm 2 is                            intentionally not implemented; RTDR is                            uniformly faster here)  Region γ > 0, α > 1:   Sun et al. (2023, Algorithm 1) (Normal or sqrt-Gamma proposal,                                  closed-form optimal parameters)  Region γ ≤ 0:   n-dependent (the crossover at 25 is benchmarked, not theoretical;   see vignette("theory") §7 for the cost-decomposition derivation):     samples per setup ≥ 25  -> RTDR (lighter per-proposal cost)     samples per setup <  25 -> Sun Algorithm 3 (lighter setup cost) rmhn(1000, 2, 1, 1, method = "rtdr")   # always RTDR rmhn(1000, 2, 1, 1, method = "sun")    # always Sun (errors for α < 1, γ > 0)

title: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution

what: Density of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — dmhn
title: Density of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — dmhn
text: Computes probability density function (log-density) Modified Half-Normal (MHN) distribution parameters alpha, beta, gamma.

title: Density of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — dmhn
code: dmhn(x, alpha = 1, beta = 1, gamma = 0, log = FALSE)

title: Density of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — dmhn
text: x Numeric vector evaluation points. alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 1. beta Scale parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 1. gamma Location parameter (\(\gamma \R\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 0. log Logical; TRUE, log-density returned. Default: FALSE.

title: Density of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — dmhn
text: numeric vector. output length equals max(length(x), length(alpha), length(beta), length(gamma)); input recycled length following standard R recycling rules. x < 0, density 0 (-Inf log = TRUE).

title: Density of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — dmhn
text: MHN density $$f(x \mid \alpha, \beta, \gamma) =   \frac{2 \beta^{\alpha/2} x^{\alpha-1}   \exp(-\beta x^2 + \gamma x)}{\Psi[\alpha/2, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]}   \quad (x > 0)$$ \(\Psi[, z]\) Fox-Wright Psi function (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 1a). default parameters alpha = 1, beta = 1, gamma = 0 correspond half-normal distribution \(\mathrm{HN}(1/\sqrt{2})\). Special cases detected dispatched closed-form solutions: \(\gamma = 0\): sqrt-Gamma distribution \(\alpha = 1\): truncated normal distribution Computation performed log-space avoid numerical underflow/overflow. alpha, beta, gamma vector, density evaluated element-wise. Fox-Wright \(\Psi\) normalizing constant recomputed consecutive elements present different \((\alpha, \beta, \gamma)\) triple, passing grouped parameters significantly faster calling dmhn inside R loop.

title: Density of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — dmhn
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536.

title: Density of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — dmhn
code: x <- seq(0, 5, length.out = 100) plot(x, dmhn(x, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1), type = "l")   # Log-density dmhn(1, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, log = TRUE) #> [1] -0.3112403

what: mhn: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn-package
title: mhn: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn-package
text: Provides density (dmhn), distribution function (pmhn), quantile function (qmhn), random generation (rmhn), moments / mode Modified Half-Normal (MHN) distribution.

title: mhn: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn-package
text: MHN distribution probability density function $$f(x \mid \alpha, \beta, \gamma) =   \frac{2\beta^{\alpha/2} x^{\alpha-1}   \exp(-\beta x^2 + \gamma x)}{\Psi[\alpha/2, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]}$$ \(x > 0\), \(\Psi[, z]\) Fox-Wright Psi function.

title: mhn: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn-package
text: alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). Controls \(x^{\alpha-1}\) term. beta Scale (rate) parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). Controls \(\exp(-\beta x^2)\) term. gamma Location (skewness) parameter (\(\gamma \R\)). Controls \(\exp(\gamma x)\) term.

title: mhn: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn-package
text: \(\gamma = 0\) Square-root Gamma distribution (\(X^2 \sim \textrm{Gamma}(\alpha/2, \beta)\)) (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 6a). \(\alpha = 1\) Truncated normal distribution \(\textrm{TN}(\gamma/(2\beta), 1/\sqrt{2\beta}, 0, \infty)\) (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 6b). \(\alpha = 1, \gamma = 0\) Half-normal distribution \(\textrm{HN}(1/\sqrt{2\beta})\) (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 6c).

title: mhn: The Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn-package

what: Excess Kurtosis of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_kurtosis
title: Excess Kurtosis of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_kurtosis
text: Computes excess kurtosis \(\gamma_2 = E[(X - \mu)^4] / \sigma^4 - 3\) \(X \sim \mathrm{MHN}(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)\).

title: Excess Kurtosis of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_kurtosis
code: mhn_kurtosis(alpha, beta, gamma)

title: Excess Kurtosis of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_kurtosis
text: alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). beta Scale parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). gamma Location parameter (\(\gamma \R\)).

title: Excess Kurtosis of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_kurtosis
text: numeric scalar.

title: Excess Kurtosis of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_kurtosis
text: Uses moment recurrence (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 2b) compute raw moments fourth order, converts central moments.

title: Excess Kurtosis of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_kurtosis
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536. (Lemma 2b)

title: Excess Kurtosis of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_kurtosis
code: mhn_kurtosis(alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 0) #> [1] 0.2450893

what: Mean of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mean
title: Mean of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mean
text: Computes \(E(X)\) \(X \sim \mathrm{MHN}(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)\).

title: Mean of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mean
code: mhn_mean(alpha, beta, gamma)

title: Mean of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mean
text: alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). beta Scale parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). gamma Location parameter (\(\gamma \R\)).

title: Mean of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mean
text: numeric scalar.

title: Mean of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mean
text: mean computed ratio Fox-Wright Psi functions: $$E(X) = \frac{\Psi[(\alpha+1)/2,\, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]}{   \sqrt{\beta}\, \Psi[\alpha/2,\, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]}$$

title: Mean of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mean
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536. (Lemma 2a)

title: Mean of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mean
code: mhn_mean(alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 0) #> [1] 0.8862269

what: Mode of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mode
title: Mode of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mode
text: Computes mode (probable value) MHN distribution.

title: Mode of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mode
code: mhn_mode(alpha, beta, gamma)

title: Mode of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mode
text: alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). beta Scale parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). gamma Location parameter (\(\gamma \R\)).

title: Mode of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mode
text: numeric scalar. Returns NA interior mode exists (density monotonically decreasing \((0, \infty)\)).

title: Mode of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mode
text: mode depends \(\alpha\): \(\alpha > 1\) \((\gamma + \sqrt{\gamma^2 + 8\beta(\alpha - 1)}) / (4\beta)\) (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 3b). \(\alpha = 1\) \(\max(0, \gamma / (2\beta))\), obtained mode truncated normal \(\mathrm{TN}(\gamma/(2\beta), 1/\sqrt{2\beta}, 0,     \infty)\) MHN reduces case (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 6b). \(0 < \alpha < 1\) interior mode exists \(\gamma > 0\) \(\alpha \geq 1 - \gamma^2 / (8\beta)\) (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 3c); otherwise density monotonically decreasing (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 3d) NA returned.

title: Mode of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mode
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536. (Lemma 3b–d, Lemma 6b)

title: Mode of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_mode
code: mhn_mode(alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) #> [1] 1 mhn_mode(alpha = 1, beta = 1, gamma = 2) #> [1] 1 mhn_mode(alpha = 0.5, beta = 1, gamma = -1)  # NA #> [1] NA

code: mhn_skewness(alpha, beta, gamma)

title: Skewness of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_skewness
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536. (Lemma 2b)

what: Variance of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_var
title: Variance of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_var
text: Computes \(\mathrm{Var}(X)\) \(X \sim \mathrm{MHN}(\alpha, \beta, \gamma)\).

title: Variance of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_var
code: mhn_var(alpha, beta, gamma)

title: Variance of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_var
text: alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). beta Scale parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). gamma Location parameter (\(\gamma \R\)).

title: Variance of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_var
text: numeric scalar.

title: Variance of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_var
text: Uses formula (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 2c): $$\mathrm{Var}(X) = \frac{\alpha}{2\beta} +   E(X)\left(\frac{\gamma}{2\beta} - E(X)\right)$$ \(\alpha \geq 1\), variance satisfies \(\mathrm{Var}(X) \leq 1/(2\beta)\) (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 4c).

title: Variance of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_var
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536. (Lemma 2c)

title: Variance of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — mhn_var
code: mhn_var(alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 0) #> [1] 0.2146018

what: Distribution Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — pmhn
title: Distribution Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — pmhn
text: Computes cumulative distribution function (CDF) Modified Half-Normal (MHN) distribution parameters alpha, beta, gamma.

title: Distribution Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — pmhn
code: pmhn(q, alpha = 1, beta = 1, gamma = 0, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)

title: Distribution Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — pmhn
text: q Numeric vector quantiles. alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 1. beta Scale parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 1. gamma Location parameter (\(\gamma \R\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 0. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\), otherwise \(P(X > q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. Default: FALSE.

title: Distribution Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — pmhn
text: numeric vector. output length equals max(length(q), length(alpha), length(beta), length(gamma)); input recycled length following standard R recycling rules. q <= 0 CDF 0; q = Inf 1.

title: Distribution Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — pmhn
text: CDF computed via series representation $$F(x \mid \alpha, \beta, \gamma) =   \frac{1}{\Psi[\alpha/2, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]}   \sum_{=0}^{\infty} \frac{z^}{!}\, \Gamma(s_i)\, P(s_i, \beta x^2)$$ \(z = \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}\), \(s_i = (\alpha + )/2\), \(P(s, y)\) regularized lower incomplete gamma function (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 1b; equivalent paper's form via identity \(\Gamma(s)\, P(s, y) = \gamma(s, y)\), \(\gamma(s, y)\) lower incomplete gamma function used paper).  infinite sum truncated constructive bound \(K = \max\{K_1, K_2\}\) Sun et al. (2023), Supplementary Lemma 10(d), makes truncation residual bounded user's tolerance divided \(\Psi\).  double-precision cancellation alternating-sign accumulator \(\gamma < 0\) exceed tolerance, series replaced Gauss-Kronrod (tanh-sinh \(\alpha < 1\)) numerical integration density \([0, q]\). Special cases detected dispatched standard R primitives: \(\gamma = 0\): pgamma(q^2, alpha/2, scale = 1/beta) \(\alpha = 1\): truncated-normal CDF via pnorm alpha, beta, gamma vector, CDF evaluated element-wise.  Fox-Wright \(\Psi\) normalizing constant recomputed consecutive elements present different \((\alpha, \beta, \gamma)\) triple, passing grouped parameters significantly faster calling pmhn inside R loop.

title: Distribution Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — pmhn
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536.

title: Distribution Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — pmhn
code: # Basic evaluation pmhn(c(0.5, 1, 1.5), alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) #> [1] 0.1129101 0.4338796 0.7614259  # Tail / log forms pmhn(2, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.06369619 pmhn(2, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, log.p = TRUE) #> [1] -0.06581527  # Special case: gamma = 0 reduces to sqrt-Gamma all.equal(pmhn(1.5, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 0),           pgamma(1.5^2, shape = 1, rate = 1)) #> [1] TRUE

what: Quantile Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — qmhn
title: Quantile Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — qmhn
text: Computes quantile (inverse cumulative) function Modified Half-Normal (MHN) distribution parameters alpha, beta, gamma.

title: Quantile Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — qmhn
code: qmhn(p, alpha = 1, beta = 1, gamma = 0, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)

title: Quantile Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — qmhn
text: p Numeric vector probabilities. alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 1. beta Scale parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 1. gamma Location parameter (\(\gamma \R\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 0. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\), otherwise \(P(X > q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities provided log scale. Default: FALSE.

title: Quantile Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — qmhn
text: numeric vector. output length equals max(length(p), length(alpha), length(beta), length(gamma)); input recycled length following standard R recycling rules. qmhn(0) = 0 qmhn(1) = Inf. Probabilities outside \([0, 1]\) yield NaN.

title: Quantile Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — qmhn
text: general case, \(q = F^{-1}(p)\) obtained TOMS 748 root-finder applied series CDF (Sun et al., 2023, Lemma 1b). initial bracket \([\sqrt{\epsilon},\; E(X) + 8 \sqrt{\mathrm{Var}(X)}]\) doubled right (30 times) brackets target probability. Special cases detected dispatched standard R primitives: \(\gamma = 0\): sqrt(qgamma(p, alpha/2, scale = 1/beta)) \(\alpha = 1\): truncated-normal inverse via qnorm alpha, beta, gamma vector, quantile evaluated element-wise.  Fox-Wright \(\Psi\) normalizing constant moments \(E(X)\), \(\mathrm{Var}(X)\) (used size root-finder bracket) recomputed consecutive elements present different \((\alpha, \beta, \gamma)\) triple.

title: Quantile Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — qmhn
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536.

title: Quantile Function of the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — qmhn
code: # Basic evaluation qmhn(c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9), alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1) #> [1] 0.4717434 1.0906276 1.8480472  # Round-trip: F(F^-1(p)) ~ p p <- c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95) all.equal(pmhn(qmhn(p, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1),                alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1),           p, tolerance = 1e-6) #> [1] TRUE  # Tail / log forms qmhn(0.95, alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, lower.tail = FALSE) #> [1] 0.3394014 qmhn(log(0.05), alpha = 2, beta = 1, gamma = 1, log.p = TRUE) #> [1] 0.3394014

what: Random Generation from the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — rmhn
title: Random Generation from the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — rmhn
text: Draws random variates Modified Half-Normal (MHN) distribution parameters alpha, beta, gamma.

title: Random Generation from the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — rmhn
code: rmhn(n, alpha = 1, beta = 1, gamma = 0, method = c("auto", "rtdr", "sun"))

title: Random Generation from the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — rmhn
text: n Non-negative integer giving number variates draw. n = 0 returns numeric(0). alpha Shape parameter (\(\alpha > 0\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 1. beta Scale parameter (\(\beta > 0\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 1. gamma Location parameter (\(\gamma \R\)). Scalar numeric vector. Default: 0. method Sampling algorithm. One "auto" (default), "rtdr", "sun". See Details.

title: Random Generation from the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — rmhn
text: numeric vector length n. alpha, beta, gamma (recycling length n) NA non-finite (Inf, -Inf, NaN), corresponding output element NA.

title: Random Generation from the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — rmhn
text: MHN density $$f(x \mid \alpha, \beta, \gamma) =   \frac{2 \beta^{\alpha/2} x^{\alpha-1}   \exp(-\beta x^2 + \gamma x)}{\Psi[\alpha/2, \gamma/\sqrt{\beta}]}   \quad (x > 0)$$ \(\Psi[, z]\) Fox-Wright Psi function. rmhn evaluate \(\Psi\); rejection-sampling kernels cancel . default parameters alpha = 1, beta = 1, gamma = 0 correspond half-normal distribution \(\mathrm{HN}(1/\sqrt{2})\). method argument selects rejection sampler: "auto": Special-case shortcuts applicable (\(\gamma \approx 0\) -> sqrt-Gamma, \(\alpha \approx 1\) -> truncated normal). Otherwise dispatches RTDR (Gao & Wang, 2025). "rtdr": Force Relaxed Transformed Density Rejection method Gao & Wang (2025). acceptance probability bounded \(1/e \approx 0.368\) uniformly parameter space. Note: Gao & Wang (2025) use parameterization \((\lambda, \alpha, \beta)\) density proportional \(x^{\lambda - 1} \exp(-\alpha x^2 - \beta x)\); mapping Sun et al. parameterization used \(\lambda \leftrightarrow \alpha\), \(\alpha \leftrightarrow \beta\), \(\beta \leftrightarrow -\gamma\) (sign flip linear term). "sun": Force Sun et al. (2023) algorithms. Algorithm 1 used \(\gamma > 0\) \(\alpha > 1\); Algorithm 3 used \(\gamma \le 0\). combination \(\alpha < 1\) \(\gamma > 0\) unsupported triggers error. Vector parameters recycled length n following standard R rules. Trailing parameter elements beyond index n - 1 silently ignored, matching convention rnorm. Internally setup state chosen sampler reused long consecutive \((\alpha, \beta, \gamma)\) triples equal, passing parameters grouped triple faster calling rmhn inside R loop.

title: Random Generation from the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — rmhn
text: Sun, J., Kong, M., & Pal, S. (2023). Modified-Half-Normal distribution: Properties efficient sampling scheme. Communications Statistics - Theory Methods, 52(5), 1507–1536. Gao, F. & Wang, H.-B. (2025). Generating modified-half-normal random variates relaxed transformed density rejection method. Communications Statistics - Simulation Computation. Robert, C. P. (1995). Simulation truncated normal variables. Statistics Computing, 5(2), 121–125.

title: Random Generation from the Modified Half-Normal Distribution — rmhn
mhn 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: mhn 0.1.0
text: dmhn(), pmhn(), qmhn(), rmhn() provide density, distribution, quantile, random generation Modified Half-Normal (MHN) distribution Sun, Kong & Pal (2023). four functions vectorised evaluation argument parameters alpha, beta, gamma, following standard R recycling rules. ParamCache reuses Fox–Wright Psi normalising constant across consecutive elements share (alpha, beta, gamma) triple, grouped inputs evaluated significantly faster calling functions inside R loop.

title: mhn 0.1.0
text: rmhn(..., method = "auto") (default) routes parameter triple cheapest provably-correct sampler: closed-form shortcuts special cases, Sun et al. (2023, Algorithms 1 3) win, Gao & Wang (2025) Relaxed Transformed Density Rejection (RTDR) sampler elsewhere. method = "rtdr" forces RTDR uniform 1/e acceptance bound. method = "sun" forces Sun Algorithm 1 (gamma > 0, alpha > 1) Algorithm 3 (gamma <= 0); Sun Algorithm 2 intentionally implemented unsupported combination triggers clear error.

title: mhn 0.1.0
text: pmhn() uses Sun et al. (2023, Lemma 1b) series log space, truncated Sun et al. (2023, Supplementary Lemma 10(d)) constructive bound K = max(K1, K2); truncation residual bounded user’s tolerance divided Psi. gamma < 0 series uses sign-separated log-sum-exp + log- diff-exp accumulation runtime cancellation guard derived double-precision precision floor: relative cancellation loss exceed user’s tolerance, pmhn() falls back peak-normalised Boost.Math quadrature (Gauss-Kronrod alpha >= 1, tanh-sinh alpha < 1) unnormalised density. qmhn() inverts pmhn() via boost::math::tools::toms748_solve bracket [sqrt(eps), E(X) + 8 sqrt(Var(X))], doubling upper end needed.

title: mhn 0.1.0
text: mhn_mean(), mhn_var(), mhn_skewness(), mhn_kurtosis(), mhn_mode() evaluate closed-form / recurrence-based expressions Sun et al. (2023, Lemmas 2 3).

title: mhn 0.1.0
text: testthat suite > 1,700 expectations covering goodness--fit (Kolmogorov-Smirnov), special-case identities, vectorised recycling, NA / NaN propagation, Sun / Gao & Wang acceptance bounds. vignette("introduction", package = "mhn") walks every exported function runnable examples. vignette("theory", package = "mhn") theoretical companion: covers MHN family special cases, Fox–Wright Psi normalising constant, Algorithms 1 3 Sun et al. (2023), four-region Gao & Wang RTDR construction, rmhn(method = "auto") decision tree. citation("mhn") returns three bibentry objects: package, Sun et al. (2023) paper, Gao & Wang (2025) paper.
